brief: Highly venomous arachnid responsible for thousands of deaths annually in India. Identified in Hadith as one of the creatures permissible to kill even in Haram.
description: The Indian Red Scorpion (Mesobuthus tamulus) is one of the most lethal scorpion species in the world. Found across the Indian subcontinent, it is responsible for 30% of global scorpion-related fatalities. Its neurotoxic venom can cause pulmonary edema, cardiovascular collapse, and death within hours if untreated.
habitat: Rocky terrain, crevices, under stones, inside shoes and clothing in homes
diet: Insects, spiders, small lizards
lifecycle: Viviparous, 2-6 year lifespan, nocturnal predator
india_distribution: Rajasthan, Maharashtra, Andhra Pradesh, Tamil Nadu, Karnataka, Saurashtra (Gujarat)
hadith_citation: Sahih Muslim 1198; Sahih al-Bukhari 1828
hadith_arabic: خَمْسٌ مِنَ الدَّوَابِّ كُلُّهُنَّ فَاسِقٌ يُقْتَلْنَ فِي الْحَرَمِ: الْغُرَابُ وَالْحِدَأَةُ وَالْعَقْرَبُ وَالْفَأْرَةُ وَالْكَلْبُ الْعَقُورُ
hadith_translation: Five are the vicious and harmful creatures which may be killed even in the state of Ihram: the crow, the kite, the scorpion, the mouse, and the rabid dog.
quran_reference: Surah Al-An'am 6:38 — 'There is no creature on earth, nor a bird that flies with its wings, but they are communities like you.'

madhab_rulings.hanafi: Permissible to kill at all times including in Ihram and in prayer. No fidyah required.
madhab_rulings.maliki: Unanimously permissible to kill. Considered fasiq (harmful creature).
madhab_rulings.shafii: Permissible to kill even in Haram. Recommended to kill on sight due to danger.
madhab_rulings.hanbali: Killing is permissible and encouraged. Part of the five creatures named by the Prophet ﷺ.

incidence_rate: 1.2 million stings/year globally
prevention_methods: Seal cracks and crevices in walls and floors
Shake out shoes, clothing, and bedding before use
Use UV blacklight to detect scorpions at night
Clear debris, stones, and woodpiles around the house
Install door sweeps and weather stripping
Use smooth-sided containers as barriers around bed legs
removal_methods: Capture with long tongs and relocate far from home
Sticky traps in common entry points
Diatomaceous earth around perimeter
Professional pest control for infestations
PROHIBITED: Do not burn or use fire
PROHIBITED: Do not torture or mutilate

spiritual_protection.dua: أَعُوذُ بِكَلِمَاتِ اللَّهِ التَّامَّاتِ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا خَلَقَ
spiritual_protection.dua_translation: I seek refuge in the perfect words of Allah from the evil of what He has created.
spiritual_protection.prophetic_practice: The Prophet ﷺ permitted killing scorpions during Salah (Abu Dawud 921). Recite the Mu'awwidhatayn before sleep.

climate_future: Rising temperatures in Rajasthan and the Deccan Plateau are expanding scorpion habitats northward. Climate models project a 15-25% increase in scorpion sting incidence by 2050. Urban heat islands and construction in semi-arid zones are bringing scorpions into closer contact with human settlements.

faq.q: Are all scorpion stings fatal?
faq.a: No. Of 2,500+ species, only about 25 have venom lethal to humans. In India, Mesobuthus tamulus (Indian Red Scorpion) is the most dangerous.

faq.q: What is the Islamic ruling on killing scorpions?
faq.a: It is permissible (mubah) to kill scorpions at all times, including in the state of Ihram and during Salah, as explicitly stated in Sahih Muslim and Sahih al-Bukhari.

faq.q: What first aid should be given for a scorpion sting?
faq.a: Apply ice to the sting site, keep the victim calm, immobilize the stung limb, and transport to a hospital immediately. Do not apply tourniquets or attempt to suck venom.

faq.q: When are scorpions most active?
faq.a: Scorpions are nocturnal and most active during warm months (March-October in India). They seek shelter in homes during extreme heat or monsoon.

faq.q: Can scorpions be kept away naturally?
faq.a: Yes — seal entry points, remove debris, use lavender or cedar essential oils as deterrents, and keep chickens or cats which are natural scorpion predators.

emergency_contacts.nearest_antivenom: Government hospitals in endemic districts stock Polyvalent Anti-Scorpion Venom Serum (ASVS)

sources: Journal of Venomous Animals and Toxins

latin_name: Serpentes (Big Four: Naja naja, Daboia russelii, Echis carinatus, Bungarus caeruleus)
brief: India accounts for nearly half of all snakebite deaths worldwide. The 'Big Four' species are responsible for the vast majority of fatalities.
description: India's 'Big Four' venomous snakes — Indian Cobra (Naja naja), Russell's Viper (Daboia russelii), Saw-scaled Viper (Echis carinatus), and Common Krait (Bungarus caeruleus) — cause over 58,000 deaths annually. Snakebite is a neglected tropical disease affecting rural agricultural communities disproportionately.
habitat: Agricultural fields, paddy lands, stone walls, wood piles, near water sources
diet: Rodents, frogs, lizards, birds, eggs
lifecycle: Oviparous/ovoviviparous, 10-25 year lifespan depending on species
india_distribution: Throughout India, highest density in Maharashtra, West Bengal, Andhra Pradesh, Tamil Nadu, Uttar Pradesh, Bihar, Rajasthan
hadith_citation: Sahih al-Bukhari 3297, 3310; Sahih Muslim 2233
hadith_arabic: اقْتُلُوا الْحَيَّاتِ فَإِنَّ مَنْ تَرَكَهُنَّ خَشْيَةَ ثَائِرِهِنَّ فَلَيْسَ مِنَّا
hadith_translation: Kill snakes, for whoever leaves them out of fear of their retaliation is not one of us.
quran_reference: Surah Al-An'am 6:38

madhab_rulings.hanafi: Permissible to kill venomous snakes. House snakes (jinn form) should be warned three times before killing.
madhab_rulings.maliki: Permissible to kill. Recommended to warn house snakes (dhawat al-buyut) three times.
madhab_rulings.shafii: All harmful snakes may be killed. House snakes should be given three days' warning.
madhab_rulings.hanbali: Permissible to kill venomous snakes immediately. House snakes warned three times as per Hadith.

health_risks: Acute kidney injury and renal failure
incidence_rate: 5.4 million bites/year globally, 2.7 million envenomations
prevention_methods: Use a torch/flashlight when walking at night
Wear closed-toe shoes and long pants in rural areas
Sleep on elevated beds with mosquito nets tucked in
Clear vegetation and debris around the house
Store food in sealed containers to avoid attracting rodents (snake prey)
Seal holes in walls and foundations
removal_methods: Call a trained snake rescuer (not a snake charmer)
Do not attempt to handle or kill the snake yourself
Keep a safe distance and monitor the snake's location
Use a long stick to guide non-venomous snakes out
PROHIBITED: Do not burn snakes alive
PROHIBITED: Do not use cruel traps

spiritual_protection.dua: أَعُوذُ بِكَلِمَاتِ اللَّهِ التَّامَّاتِ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا خَلَقَ
spiritual_protection.dua_translation: I seek refuge in the perfect words of Allah from the evil of what He has created.
spiritual_protection.prophetic_practice: The Prophet ﷺ commanded killing snakes, especially the two-striped and short-tailed ones (Sahih al-Bukhari 3297). Recite Ayat al-Kursi and the Mu'awwidhatayn before sleep.

climate_future: Climate change is expanding snake habitats into higher altitudes and urban areas. Flooding events push snakes into homes. Models predict 15-30% increase in snakebite incidence in South Asia by 2050 due to changing monsoon patterns and agricultural expansion.

faq.q: What should I do immediately after a snakebite?
faq.a: Keep calm, immobilize the bitten limb, remove jewelry, and get to the nearest hospital with antivenom facility. Do NOT apply a tourniquet, cut the wound, or try to suck out venom.

faq.q: Are all snakes in India venomous?
faq.a: No. Of ~300 species in India, only about 60 are venomous. The 'Big Four' cause >90% of snakebite deaths.

faq.q: Is it permissible to kill all snakes in Islam?
faq.a: Venomous snakes may be killed. For house snakes, the Hadith recommends warning them three times before killing, as some may be jinn in serpent form.

faq.q: When is snakebite season in India?
faq.a: June to November (monsoon and post-monsoon) sees the highest snakebite incidence, with a peak during rice harvesting season.

faq.q: Where can I find antivenom in India?
faq.a: Government district hospitals and primary health centres in endemic areas stock Polyvalent Anti-Snake Venom Serum (ASVS). The national helpline is 108 (ambulance).

emergency_contacts.nearest_antivenom: Government district hospitals, PHCs in endemic districts

brief: Aggressive stinging insects capable of multiple stings. Responsible for severe allergic reactions and anaphylaxis deaths.
description: Wasps (Vespa orientalis, Polistes spp.) are aggressive social insects that build nests in and around human dwellings. Unlike bees, they can sting repeatedly. Their venom causes intense pain, and in sensitized individuals, anaphylactic shock that can be fatal within minutes.
habitat: Roof eaves, window frames, attics, tree branches near homes, underground burrows
diet: Other insects, fruit, nectar, human food scraps
lifecycle: Annual colonies, queen overwinters, workers die in autumn
india_distribution: Throughout India, especially in tropical and subtropical regions
hadith_citation: General ruling under 'harmful creatures' (mu'dhiyat) — analogized to scorpion ruling
hadith_arabic: مَا لَا يُؤْذِي فَلَا يُقْتَلُ وَمَا يُؤْذِي فَيُقْتَلُ
hadith_translation: That which does not harm should not be killed, and that which harms may be killed.
quran_reference: Surah An-Nahl 16:68-69 (reference to bees, wasps distinguished from bees in fiqh)

madhab_rulings.hanafi: Permissible to kill if they pose a threat to safety. Nest removal permissible.
madhab_rulings.maliki: Permissible to kill harmful wasps. Distinguished from honeybees which are protected.
madhab_rulings.shafii: Killing permissible when they threaten harm. Analogized to other mu'dhiyat.
madhab_rulings.hanbali: Permissible to kill aggressive wasps. Prevention of harm takes priority.

health_risks: Multiple sting syndrome (>50 stings can be lethal)
incidence_rate: Millions of stings annually worldwide
prevention_methods: Inspect and remove nests in early spring when colonies are small
Seal openings in walls, roofs, and eaves
Avoid leaving sugary foods or drinks outdoors
Wear light-colored, smooth-textured clothing
Avoid floral perfumes and bright clothing near nests
Install mesh screens on windows and doors
removal_methods: Professional pest control for large nests
Remove nests at night when wasps are dormant
Use soapy water spray to immobilize wasps
Trap wasps with sweet liquid traps away from living areas
PROHIBITED: Do not burn nests (fire hazard)
PROHIBITED: Do not spray pesticides near food areas

spiritual_protection.dua: أَعُوذُ بِكَلِمَاتِ اللَّهِ التَّامَّاتِ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا خَلَقَ
spiritual_protection.dua_translation: I seek refuge in the perfect words of Allah from the evil of what He has created.
spiritual_protection.prophetic_practice: Recite morning and evening adhkar for protection from harmful creatures.

climate_future: Warmer winters are allowing wasp colonies to survive longer and grow larger. Urbanization provides more nesting sites. Invasive species like Vespa velutina are expanding range in South Asia.

faq.q: How is a wasp different from a bee?
faq.a: Wasps have smooth bodies, can sting multiple times, are more aggressive, and do not produce honey. Bees are fuzzy, sting once (and die), and are generally docile unless provoked.

faq.q: Is killing a wasp the same ruling as killing a bee?
faq.a: No. Bees are generally protected in Islamic jurisprudence due to their benefit (honey). Wasps are classified as harmful (mu'dhi) and may be killed.

faq.q: What should I do if stung by a wasp?
faq.a: Remove the stinger if present, wash with soap and water, apply ice, take antihistamines. Seek emergency care if you experience difficulty breathing, swelling of face/throat, or dizziness.

faq.q: When should I be worried about a wasp nest near my home?
faq.a: Any nest within 3 meters of living areas, especially near doorways, windows, or children's play areas, should be removed by a professional.

faq.q: Can wasps be beneficial?
faq.a: Yes, wasps are natural pest controllers, feeding on caterpillars, flies, and other crop pests. If nests are away from living areas, they can be left alone.

emergency_contacts.nearest_antivenom: Not applicable (no specific antivenom; treat anaphylaxis with epinephrine)

sources: Journal of Allergy and Clinical Immunology

brief: Venomous arthropod with painful bite. Common in tropical Indian homes, especially during monsoon season.
description: The Giant Centipede (Scolopendra subspinipes) is found throughout tropical India. Its venomous forcipules deliver an extremely painful bite that can cause swelling, fever, and in rare cases, severe allergic reactions. They are nocturnal hunters that enter homes seeking prey and moisture.
habitat: Under stones, logs, leaf litter, bathrooms, damp basements
diet: Insects, spiders, small vertebrates
lifecycle: 3-7 year lifespan, maternal care of eggs
india_distribution: Western Ghats, Northeastern states, Kerala, Karnataka, Tamil Nadu
hadith_citation: Classified under harmful creatures (mu'dhiyat) by analogy
hadith_arabic: مَا لَا يُؤْذِي فَلَا يُقْتَلُ وَمَا يُؤْذِي فَيُقْتَلُ
hadith_translation: That which does not harm should not be killed, and that which harms may be killed.
quran_reference: Surah Al-An'am 6:38

madhab_rulings.hanafi: Permissible to kill as a harmful creature (mu'dhi).
madhab_rulings.maliki: Permissible to kill. Falls under general ruling on harmful creatures.
madhab_rulings.shafii: Permissible to kill due to harm (dharar).
madhab_rulings.hanbali: Permissible to kill. Analogized to scorpion ruling.

incidence_rate: Thousands of bites annually in tropical regions
prevention_methods: Reduce moisture in bathrooms and basements
Seal cracks in walls and floors
Remove leaf litter and debris near the house
Use dehumidifiers in damp areas
Check shoes and clothing before wearing
Install door sweeps
removal_methods: Capture with tongs and relocate
Sticky traps in damp areas
Diatomaceous earth in entry points
Reduce prey insects to discourage centipedes
PROHIBITED: Do not use fire
PROHIBITED: Do not use excessive pesticides indoors

spiritual_protection.dua: أَعُوذُ بِكَلِمَاتِ اللَّهِ التَّامَّاتِ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا خَلَقَ
spiritual_protection.dua_translation: I seek refuge in the perfect words of Allah from the evil of what He has created.
spiritual_protection.prophetic_practice: Recite evening adhkar and seek refuge before sleep.

climate_future: Increased rainfall and humidity from climate change are expanding centipede habitats. Urban flooding brings centipedes into homes more frequently. Tropical species are moving to higher elevations.

faq.q: Are centipede bites dangerous?
faq.a: While extremely painful, centipede bites are rarely fatal. Children, elderly, and allergic individuals are at higher risk of complications.

faq.q: Why do centipedes come inside homes?
faq.a: They seek moisture, warmth, and prey. Bathrooms, basements, and kitchens are common entry areas, especially during monsoon.

faq.q: How do I treat a centipede bite?
faq.a: Wash the area, apply hot water (not scalding) to break down venom proteins, use ice for swelling, and take pain medication. See a doctor if symptoms worsen.

faq.q: What is the difference between a centipede and a millipede?
faq.a: Centipedes are venomous predators with one pair of legs per segment. Millipedes are harmless detritivores with two pairs per segment.

faq.q: Can I kill centipedes in Islam?
faq.a: Yes, centipedes are classified as harmful creatures (mu'dhiyat) and may be killed when they enter homes and pose a threat.

brief: Medically significant spiders including Redback and Recluse species found in Indian homes.
description: While most spiders in India are harmless, several species including the Indian Redback Spider (Latrodectus hasselti) and Brown Recluse relatives (Loxosceles spp.) can deliver medically significant bites causing necrosis, systemic illness, and rarely death.
habitat: Dark corners, storage areas, garages, woodpiles, outdoor toilets
diet: Insects, other spiders
lifecycle: 1-3 year lifespan, egg sacs with hundreds of spiderlings
india_distribution: Throughout India, Redback concentrated in Western and Southern India
hadith_citation: Classified under harmful creatures by analogy. Distinguished from harmless spiders in Surah Al-Ankabut.
hadith_arabic: مَا لَا يُؤْذِي فَلَا يُقْتَلُ وَمَا يُؤْذِي فَيُقْتَلُ
hadith_translation: That which does not harm should not be killed, and that which harms may be killed.
quran_reference: Surah Al-Ankabut 29:41 — 'The example of those who take allies other than Allah is like that of the spider who takes a home.'

madhab_rulings.hanafi: Venomous spiders may be killed as mu'dhiyat. Harmless spiders should not be killed.
madhab_rulings.maliki: Permissible to kill venomous spiders. Harmless spiders are makruh to kill.
madhab_rulings.shafii: Distinguished between harmful and harmless. Only harmful spiders may be killed.
madhab_rulings.hanbali: Venomous spiders permissible to kill. Harmless ones should be left alone.

health_risks: Latrodectism: severe pain, muscle spasms, sweating
Rare fatalities in children and elderly
incidence_rate: Thousands of significant spider bites annually in India
prevention_methods: Declutter storage areas and garages
Shake out shoes, gloves, and stored clothing
Use sealed plastic containers instead of cardboard boxes
Clear webs regularly from dark corners
Install screens on windows and doors
Reduce outdoor lighting that attracts prey insects
removal_methods: Capture in a jar and relocate outdoors
Vacuum webs and egg sacs
Sticky traps in dark corners
Professional pest control for infestations
PROHIBITED: Do not use fire
PROHIBITED: Do not use broad-spectrum pesticides indoors

spiritual_protection.dua: أَعُوذُ بِكَلِمَاتِ اللَّهِ التَّامَّاتِ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا خَلَقَ
spiritual_protection.dua_translation: I seek refuge in the perfect words of Allah from the evil of what He has created.
spiritual_protection.prophetic_practice: Morning and evening adhkar for protection.

climate_future: Warmer temperatures and urbanization are increasing spider-human encounters. Some tropical species are expanding northward. Climate change may alter prey availability, pushing spiders into homes.

faq.q: How do I identify a venomous spider?
faq.a: Redback: black with red/orange stripe on abdomen. Recluse: brown with violin-shaped marking. When in doubt, leave the spider alone and consult an expert.

faq.q: What's the difference between the Islamic ruling on venomous vs harmless spiders?
faq.a: Venomous spiders are classified as mu'dhiyat (harmful) and may be killed. Harmless spiders should be left alone or relocated, as the spider is mentioned in the Qur'an (Surah Al-Ankabut).

faq.q: Should I kill every spider I see?
faq.a: No. Most spiders are beneficial, eating mosquitoes and other pests. Only venomous species posing a direct threat should be dealt with.

faq.q: What first aid for a spider bite?
faq.a: Wash the bite, apply ice, keep the limb elevated. For suspected Redback or Recluse bites, seek medical attention immediately.

faq.q: Are spider webs in the home a concern?
faq.a: Most house spiders are harmless. Regular cleaning removes webs and egg sacs. Concern is warranted only if you identify venomous species.

emergency_contacts.nearest_antivenom: Redback antivenom available at major hospitals

latin_name: Aedes aegypti / Anopheles stephensi / Culex quinquefasciatus
brief: The deadliest animal on Earth. Transmits Malaria, Dengue, Chikungunya, Japanese Encephalitis, and Zika. Kills 600,000+ people annually worldwide.
description: Mosquitoes are the deadliest animals on Earth. Three genera dominate India: Anopheles (malaria), Aedes (dengue, chikungunya, Zika), and Culex (Japanese encephalitis, filariasis). India bears 4% of global malaria burden and reports hundreds of thousands of dengue cases annually.
habitat: Stagnant water, flower pots, tyres, water tanks, drains, any water-collecting container
diet: Female: blood meal for egg production. Male: nectar and plant juices
lifecycle: Egg → Larva → Pupa → Adult. 7-14 day cycle. Adults live 2-4 weeks.
india_distribution: Entire country, with species-specific distribution patterns
hadith_citation: Sunan Ibn Majah 3228; general mu'dhiyat ruling
hadith_arabic: إِذَا وَقَعَ الذُّبَابُ فِي شَرَابِ أَحَدِكُمْ فَلْيَغْمِسْهُ ثُمَّ لِيَنْزِعْهُ
hadith_translation: The Prophet ﷺ mentioned the mosquito among the smallest of Allah's creatures, yet it can carry disease. Killing harmful insects is permissible.
quran_reference: Surah Al-Baqarah 2:26 — 'Indeed, Allah is not timid to present an example — that of a mosquito or what is smaller than it.'

madhab_rulings.hanafi: Permissible to kill as a disease-carrying harmful creature.
madhab_rulings.maliki: Permissible to kill. Falls under mu'dhiyat classification.
madhab_rulings.shafii: Permissible to kill. Prevention of disease is obligatory (wajib).
madhab_rulings.hanbali: Permissible to kill. Protection of health is a maqasid al-shariah priority.

health_risks: Malaria (Anopheles): fever, anemia, cerebral malaria, death
Chikungunya (Aedes): severe joint pain, chronic arthralgia
Japanese Encephalitis (Culex): brain inflammation, neurological damage
incidence_rate: India: ~5 million malaria cases, 100,000+ dengue cases annually
prevention_methods: Eliminate stagnant water sources (flower pots, tyres, coolers, tanks)
Use insecticide-treated bed nets (ITNs/LLINs)
Apply mosquito repellent (DEET, picaridin)
Install mesh screens on all windows and doors
Use indoor residual spraying (IRS) in endemic areas
Wear long-sleeved, light-colored clothing at dusk and dawn
removal_methods: Larviciding of water bodies with BTI (Bacillus thuringiensis israelensis)
Fogging in outbreak areas (by municipal authorities)
Mosquito traps (CO2, UV, or chemical lure)
Introduce larvivorous fish (Gambusia, Guppy) in water storage
PROHIBITED: Do not use banned pesticides (DDT in most applications)
PROHIBITED: Do not use fire or smoke in enclosed spaces

spiritual_protection.dua: أَعُوذُ بِكَلِمَاتِ اللَّهِ التَّامَّاتِ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا خَلَقَ
spiritual_protection.dua_translation: I seek refuge in the perfect words of Allah from the evil of what He has created.
spiritual_protection.prophetic_practice: The Qur'an mentions the mosquito as a sign of Allah's creation. Maintain cleanliness (taharah) and eliminate breeding sites as part of Islamic hygiene obligations.

climate_future: Global warming is expanding mosquito ranges to higher altitudes (Himalayan foothills now seeing dengue) and extending transmission seasons. Urban heat islands increase breeding. Extreme rainfall events create new breeding sites. India projected to see 20-30% increase in malaria-suitable areas by 2050.

faq.q: Why are mosquitoes mentioned in the Qur'an?
faq.a: Surah Al-Baqarah 2:26 mentions the mosquito as an example of Allah's creative power — even the smallest creature has purpose and design.

faq.q: Which diseases do different mosquito species carry?
faq.a: Anopheles: Malaria. Aedes: Dengue, Chikungunya, Zika. Culex: Japanese Encephalitis, Filariasis.

faq.q: How can I protect my family from mosquito-borne diseases?
faq.a: Use bed nets, eliminate stagnant water, apply repellent, install screens, and seek early medical attention for fever during monsoon season.

faq.q: Is killing mosquitoes permissible in Islam?
faq.a: Yes. Mosquitoes are classified as harmful (mu'dhi) and killing them is permissible across all four madhabs.

faq.q: When are mosquitoes most active in India?
faq.a: Aedes (dengue): daytime, especially dawn and dusk. Anopheles (malaria): night. Culex (JE): dusk to dawn. Peak season: monsoon and post-monsoon (June-November).

emergency_contacts.nearest_antivenom: N/A — Antimalarial and dengue treatment at government hospitals

sources: National Vector Borne Disease Control Programme

brief: Major disease vector carrying 30+ species of bacteria. Triggers asthma and allergic reactions. One of the most common household pests in India.
description: Cockroaches are among the most resilient and common household pests. They carry over 30 species of bacteria (including Salmonella and E. coli), 6 parasitic worms, and 7+ human pathogens. Their shed skin and feces are major triggers for asthma and allergies, especially in children.
habitat: Kitchens, bathrooms, drains, behind appliances, warm and damp areas
diet: Omnivorous — food scraps, grease, paper, fabric, glue, soap
lifecycle: Egg case (ootheca) → nymph → adult. 6-12 month lifecycle. Can live 1-2 years.
india_distribution: Entire country, highest density in urban and semi-urban areas
hadith_citation: Classified under harmful creatures (mu'dhiyat)
hadith_arabic: مَا لَا يُؤْذِي فَلَا يُقْتَلُ وَمَا يُؤْذِي فَيُقْتَلُ
hadith_translation: That which does not harm should not be killed, and that which harms may be killed.
quran_reference: General principle of cleanliness — Surah Al-Baqarah 2:222 'Indeed, Allah loves those who are constantly repentant and loves those who purify themselves.'

madhab_rulings.hanafi: Permissible to kill as a filthy, disease-carrying creature.
madhab_rulings.maliki: Permissible to kill. Cleanliness of home is emphasized.
madhab_rulings.shafii: Permissible to kill as mu'dhi. Part of maintaining taharah.
madhab_rulings.hanbali: Permissible to kill. Disease prevention is a priority.

health_risks: Bacterial transmission: Salmonella, E. coli, Shigella
fatality_india: Indirect: contributes to thousands of gastroenteritis deaths
fatality_world: Indirect: major contributor to diarrheal disease burden
incidence_rate: Present in ~78% of urban Indian homes
prevention_methods: Store all food in airtight containers
Clean kitchen surfaces and floors daily
Fix leaking pipes and taps immediately
Seal cracks around pipes, sinks, and baseboards
Take garbage out daily and use sealed bins
Do not leave pet food out overnight
removal_methods: Gel bait stations in kitchen and bathroom
Boric acid powder in crevices and behind appliances
Diatomaceous earth as natural desiccant
Professional pest control for severe infestations
PROHIBITED: Do not use fire
PROHIBITED: Avoid spraying pesticides directly on food surfaces

spiritual_protection.dua: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ الَّذِي لَا يَضُرُّ مَعَ اسْمِهِ شَيْءٌ
spiritual_protection.dua_translation: In the name of Allah with whose name nothing is harmed on earth or in heaven.
spiritual_protection.surah: Maintain cleanliness as per Surah Al-Muddathir 74:4 'And your clothing purify'
spiritual_protection.prophetic_practice: The Prophet ﷺ emphasized cleanliness (taharah) as half of faith. Keep homes clean to prevent pest infestation.

climate_future: Urban heat islands and warmer winters are extending cockroach breeding seasons. Increasing urbanization and slum development create ideal habitats. Climate change may increase German cockroach populations in previously cooler regions.

faq.q: Do cockroaches really carry disease?
faq.a: Yes. Cockroaches carry 30+ bacterial species, 6 parasitic worms, and multiple pathogens. They contaminate food through their feces, saliva, and shed skin.

faq.q: Why are cockroaches so hard to eliminate?
faq.a: They can survive a month without food, two weeks without water, withstand radiation, and reproduce rapidly (one female can produce 300+ offspring in her lifetime).

faq.q: Is it permissible to kill cockroaches in Islam?
faq.a: Yes. Cockroaches are classified as harmful (mu'dhi) and disease-carrying. Killing them is permissible and maintaining home cleanliness is an Islamic obligation.

faq.q: What is the most effective way to get rid of cockroaches?
faq.a: Combination of sanitation (removing food/water sources), sealing entry points, and using gel bait stations. Professional pest control for severe infestations.

faq.q: Can cockroaches cause asthma?
faq.a: Yes. Cockroach allergens (from shed skin, feces, saliva) are a major trigger for childhood asthma, especially in urban settings.

brief: Carries 100+ pathogens including cholera, typhoid, and dysentery bacteria. Mentioned specifically in the Qur'an and Hadith.
description: The common housefly (Musca domestica) carries over 100 pathogens on its body and in its gut, including bacteria causing cholera, typhoid, dysentery, and tuberculosis. It contaminates food through regurgitation, defecation, and mechanical transfer. The fly is specifically mentioned in the Qur'an (Surah Al-Hajj 22:73).
habitat: Near food, garbage, animal waste, any human settlement
diet: Liquid or liquefied food (regurgitates on solid food to dissolve it)
lifecycle: Egg → Larva (maggot) → Pupa → Adult. 7-10 day cycle. Adults live 15-25 days.
india_distribution: Entire country, ubiquitous in all human settlements
hadith_citation: Sahih al-Bukhari 3320, 5782
hadith_arabic: إِذَا وَقَعَ الذُّبَابُ فِي شَرَابِ أَحَدِكُمْ فَلْيَغْمِسْهُ ثُمَّ لِيَنْزِعْهُ فَإِنَّ فِي إِحْدَى جَنَاحَيْهِ دَاءً وَفِي الْأُخْرَى شِفَاءً
hadith_translation: If a housefly falls in the drink of anyone of you, he should dip it (in the drink) and then throw it out, for one of its wings has a disease and the other has the cure.
quran_reference: Surah Al-Hajj 22:73 — 'O people, an example is presented, so listen to it. Indeed, those you invoke besides Allah will never create even a fly, even if they gathered together for that purpose.'

madhab_rulings.hanafi: Permissible to kill. The Hadith of dipping indicates its nature as a disease carrier.
madhab_rulings.maliki: Permissible to kill. Maintaining food hygiene is obligatory.
madhab_rulings.shafii: Permissible to kill as mu'dhi. The Hadith provides scientific insight about wings.
madhab_rulings.hanbali: Permissible to kill. Cleanliness and food safety are Islamic priorities.

incidence_rate: Ubiquitous in all human settlements globally
high_risk_states: Higher risk in areas with poor sanitation
prevention_methods: Cover all food and drinks
Use mesh screens on windows and doors
Dispose of garbage promptly in sealed bins
Clean animal waste and drains regularly
Use fly traps and UV insect killers
Maintain clean kitchen and dining areas
removal_methods: Fly swatters (manual)
UV light fly traps
Vinegar and dish soap traps
Fly paper and sticky traps
PROHIBITED: Do not use excessive pesticide sprays near food
PROHIBITED: Do not burn

spiritual_protection.dua: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ
spiritual_protection.dua_translation: In the name of Allah (said before eating/drinking).
spiritual_protection.prophetic_practice: The Prophet ﷺ instructed dipping the fly if it falls in drink (Sahih al-Bukhari 3320). Modern science has confirmed antimicrobial compounds on one wing.

climate_future: Warmer temperatures accelerate fly breeding cycles. Poor sanitation infrastructure combined with urbanization increases fly populations. Climate change is expanding fly-borne disease transmission windows.

faq.q: Why is the fly mentioned in the Qur'an?
faq.a: Surah Al-Hajj 22:73 uses the fly as an example of Allah's creative power — even all of humanity cannot create something as 'simple' as a fly.

faq.q: Is the Hadith about dipping the fly scientifically valid?
faq.a: Research has found antimicrobial compounds on one wing of the fly, supporting the Hadith. However, many scholars note this is about the principle, not a medical prescription.

faq.q: How many diseases can a housefly carry?
faq.a: Over 100 pathogens including bacteria, viruses, fungi, and parasites. It is a mechanical vector, carrying pathogens on its body and in its gut.

faq.q: What is the best way to keep flies out of my kitchen?
faq.a: Mesh screens, covered food, clean surfaces, sealed garbage, and UV fly traps are the most effective combination.

faq.q: Is it a sin to kill a fly in Islam?
faq.a: No. Flies are classified as harmful (mu'dhi) and killing them is permissible. However, needless cruelty to any creature is prohibited.

emergency_contacts.emergency: For diarrheal disease: nearest PHC or hospital

brief: Blood-sucking parasite and vector of plague (Yersinia pestis), murine typhus, and tapeworm. Historically responsible for the Black Death.
description: Fleas are wingless, blood-sucking ectoparasites that have caused more human death through plague transmission than any other arthropod. The Oriental Rat Flea (Xenopsylla cheopis) transmits Yersinia pestis, which historically killed an estimated 200 million people. Fleas remain a public health concern in India.
habitat: Animal bedding, carpets, cracks in floors, pet fur, soil
diet: Blood from mammals and birds
lifecycle: Egg → Larva → Pupa → Adult. Complete cycle in 2-3 weeks under favorable conditions.
india_distribution: Throughout India, especially in areas with domestic animals and rodents
hadith_citation: Classified under harmful parasites. Cleanliness (taharah) rulings apply.
hadith_arabic: الطُّهُورُ شَطْرُ الْإِيمَانِ
hadith_translation: Cleanliness is half of faith. (Sahih Muslim 223)
quran_reference: Surah Al-Muddathir 74:4 — 'And your clothing purify.'

madhab_rulings.hanafi: Permissible to kill as a blood-sucking parasite. Removing fleas is part of personal hygiene (taharah).
madhab_rulings.maliki: Permissible to kill. Pest that causes harm to humans and animals.
madhab_rulings.shafii: Permissible to kill. Part of maintaining bodily cleanliness.
madhab_rulings.hanbali: Permissible to kill. Protection from disease-carrying parasites is obligatory.

health_risks: Anemia in heavy infestations (pets and infants)
fatality_india: Plague outbreaks rare but historically devastating; last major: Surat 1994
incidence_rate: Common in households with pets; plague cases sporadic
prevention_methods: Regularly wash and treat pet bedding
Vacuum carpets and upholstery frequently
Treat pets with veterinary-approved flea preventatives
Seal cracks in floors and baseboards
Maintain yard cleanliness to reduce rodent habitat
Wash bedding in hot water weekly
removal_methods: Veterinary flea treatment for pets (topical or oral)
Thorough vacuuming of all carpets, furniture, and cracks
Wash all bedding and fabrics in hot water
Diatomaceous earth on carpets and pet areas
PROHIBITED: Do not use pet flea products on humans
PROHIBITED: Do not use fire or smoke indoors

spiritual_protection.dua: أَعُوذُ بِكَلِمَاتِ اللَّهِ التَّامَّاتِ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا خَلَقَ
spiritual_protection.dua_translation: I seek refuge in the perfect words of Allah from the evil of what He has created.
spiritual_protection.prophetic_practice: Maintain personal and household cleanliness as emphasized in the Sunnah.

climate_future: Warmer winters allow flea populations to persist year-round. Climate change may increase rodent populations, indirectly boosting flea numbers. Urbanization and pet ownership trends increase human-flea contact.

faq.q: Can fleas live on humans?
faq.a: Human fleas (Pulex irritans) can, but cat/dog fleas prefer animal hosts. They will bite humans opportunistically but rarely infest human hair.

faq.q: How do I know if I have fleas?
faq.a: Look for small, itchy red bites in clusters (often on ankles/legs), black specks (flea dirt) on pet bedding, and jumping insects in carpet.

faq.q: Can fleas spread plague in India today?
faq.a: While rare, plague is not eradicated. The 1994 Surat outbreak showed it can re-emerge. Surveillance continues in endemic zones.

faq.q: Is killing fleas permissible in Islam?
faq.a: Yes. Fleas are blood-sucking parasites classified as harmful. Removing them is part of the Islamic obligation of cleanliness (taharah).

faq.q: How long can fleas survive without a host?
faq.a: Adult fleas can survive 1-2 weeks without blood. Pupae in cocoons can remain dormant for months, hatching when they detect vibrations or CO2.

brief: Blood-sucking arachnid transmitting Crimean-Congo Hemorrhagic Fever (CCHF) and Kyasanur Forest Disease (KFD) in India.
description: Ticks are obligate blood-feeding ectoparasites that transmit dangerous viral and bacterial diseases. In India, Hyalomma ticks transmit CCHF (Crimean-Congo Hemorrhagic Fever) and Haemaphysalis ticks transmit KFD (Kyasanur Forest Disease, 'monkey fever'). Both are high-fatality hemorrhagic fevers.
habitat: Grasslands, forests, livestock, pets, leaf litter
diet: Blood from mammals, birds, reptiles
lifecycle: Egg → Larva → Nymph → Adult. Multi-host lifecycle, 2-3 year span.
india_distribution: CCHF: Gujarat, Rajasthan. KFD: Karnataka, Kerala, Tamil Nadu, Goa
hadith_citation: Classified under harmful parasites by analogy
hadith_arabic: مَا لَا يُؤْذِي فَلَا يُقْتَلُ وَمَا يُؤْذِي فَيُقْتَلُ
hadith_translation: That which does not harm should not be killed, and that which harms may be killed.
quran_reference: General cleanliness and health protection principles

madhab_rulings.hanafi: Permissible to kill and remove from the body.
madhab_rulings.maliki: Permissible to kill. Blood-sucking parasites are harmful.
madhab_rulings.shafii: Permissible to kill and remove. Health protection is priority.
madhab_rulings.hanbali: Permissible to kill. Similar ruling to lice and fleas.

health_risks: Severe allergic reactions to tick saliva
fatality_india: CCHF: ~30% case fatality; KFD: 100-500 cases/year in Karnataka
incidence_rate: KFD: 100-500 cases/year in endemic districts
prevention_methods: Wear long pants tucked into socks in forested areas
Use DEET or permethrin-treated clothing
Check body for ticks after outdoor activities
Keep grass short around homes
Treat livestock with acaricides
Avoid sitting directly on grass or leaf litter
removal_methods: Use fine-tipped tweezers to grasp tick close to skin and pull straight out
Clean the bite area with antiseptic
Save the tick for identification
Monitor for fever/rash for 2 weeks after bite
PROHIBITED: Do not burn tick with match while attached
PROHIBITED: Do not squeeze tick body (releases pathogens)

spiritual_protection.dua: أَعُوذُ بِكَلِمَاتِ اللَّهِ التَّامَّاتِ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا خَلَقَ
spiritual_protection.dua_translation: I seek refuge in the perfect words of Allah from the evil of what He has created.
spiritual_protection.prophetic_practice: Seek refuge before entering forests and outdoor areas.

climate_future: Climate change is expanding tick habitats northward and to higher altitudes. Longer warm seasons mean extended tick activity periods. Migratory birds are spreading ticks to new regions. KFD has expanded beyond its original Karnataka focus.

faq.q: What is KFD (monkey fever)?
faq.a: Kyasanur Forest Disease is a tick-borne viral hemorrhagic fever endemic to Karnataka, now spreading to Kerala, Goa, and Tamil Nadu. It has 3-5% fatality rate.

faq.q: How do I safely remove a tick?
faq.a: Use fine-tipped tweezers, grasp close to the skin, pull straight out with steady pressure. Do NOT twist, squeeze, or burn the tick.

faq.q: Can ticks transmit disease immediately?
faq.a: Most tick-borne diseases require 24-48 hours of attachment for transmission. Early removal significantly reduces infection risk.

faq.q: Is there a vaccine for tick-borne diseases in India?
faq.a: A KFD vaccine exists and is used in endemic districts of Karnataka. There is no widely available CCHF vaccine yet.

faq.q: Can I kill ticks in Islam?
faq.a: Yes. Ticks are blood-sucking parasites classified as harmful (mu'dhi) and may be killed.

emergency_contacts.nearest_antivenom: KFD vaccine available in endemic Karnataka districts

brief: Nocturnal blood-sucking parasite causing severe sleep disruption, allergic reactions, and psychological distress. Rapidly spreading in Indian cities.
description: Bed bugs (Cimex lectularius) are nocturnal ectoparasites that feed exclusively on human blood. They hide in mattress seams, furniture cracks, and wall voids during the day. Infestations cause severe sleep disruption, allergic reactions, secondary infections from scratching, and significant psychological distress including anxiety and insomnia.
habitat: Mattress seams, bed frames, headboards, furniture joints, electrical outlets, wall cracks
diet: Human blood (exclusively)
lifecycle: Egg → 5 nymph stages → Adult. Female lays 200-500 eggs. Adults live 6-12 months.
india_distribution: All major cities, increasing in hotels, hostels, PG accommodations, railways
hadith_citation: Classified under harmful parasites
hadith_arabic: الطُّهُورُ شَطْرُ الْإِيمَانِ
hadith_translation: Cleanliness is half of faith. (Sahih Muslim 223)
quran_reference: General cleanliness principles

madhab_rulings.hanafi: Permissible to kill as a harmful blood-sucking parasite.
madhab_rulings.maliki: Permissible to kill. Maintaining clean sleeping quarters is emphasized.
madhab_rulings.shafii: Permissible to kill. Falls under parasitic mu'dhiyat.
madhab_rulings.hanbali: Permissible to kill. Sleep disruption and health harm justify killing.

fatality_world: No direct fatalities, massive quality-of-life impact
incidence_rate: Resurgent globally; estimated 20%+ of Indian urban homes affected
prevention_methods: Inspect hotel rooms and secondhand furniture before use
Encase mattresses and pillows in bed-bug-proof covers
Reduce clutter around sleeping areas
Vacuum frequently, especially around bed frames
Be cautious with secondhand clothing and luggage
Seal cracks in walls and around electrical outlets
removal_methods: Professional heat treatment (>50°C kills all stages)
Steam cleaning of mattresses and furniture
Wash all bedding at >60°C and dry on high heat
Diatomaceous earth in cracks and crevices
PROHIBITED: Do not use kerosene or fire (extremely dangerous)
PROHIBITED: Do not use agricultural pesticides indoors

spiritual_protection.dua: أَعُوذُ بِكَلِمَاتِ اللَّهِ التَّامَّاتِ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا خَلَقَ
spiritual_protection.dua_translation: I seek refuge in the perfect words of Allah from the evil of what He has created.
spiritual_protection.prophetic_practice: The Prophet ﷺ would dust off his bed before sleeping (Sahih al-Bukhari 6320). Maintain clean bedding.

climate_future: Global travel and urbanization are spreading bed bugs rapidly. Pesticide resistance is increasing. Climate change and warmer indoor environments may accelerate breeding cycles.

faq.q: How do I know if I have bed bugs?
faq.a: Look for: small blood stains on sheets, dark fecal spots on mattress seams, shed skins, musty odor, and itchy bite clusters (often in lines of 3).

faq.q: Can bed bugs transmit diseases?
faq.a: While they carry pathogens, there is no confirmed evidence of disease transmission to humans. The primary health impact is allergic reactions, sleep loss, and psychological distress.

faq.q: Are bed bugs a sign of a dirty home?
faq.a: No. Bed bugs are equal-opportunity pests found in 5-star hotels and clean homes alike. They hitchhike on luggage, clothing, and furniture.

faq.q: What is the most effective treatment?
faq.a: Professional heat treatment is the gold standard. It kills all life stages without chemicals. Combine with encasements, vacuuming, and monitoring.

faq.q: Is it permissible to kill bed bugs in Islam?
faq.a: Yes. They are blood-sucking parasites that cause harm (dharar) and killing them is permissible across all madhabs.

brief: One of the five Fawasiq named by the Prophet ﷺ. Major disease vector (Leptospirosis, Plague, Hantavirus) and property destroyer. Causes electrical fires by gnawing wires.
description: The rat is one of the five creatures explicitly named by the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ as fasiq (harmful/corrupt) that may be killed even in the sacred precincts of the Haram. Rats are vectors for over 35 diseases including Leptospirosis, Plague, and Hantavirus. They cause billions in property damage annually by gnawing through wires (causing fires), contaminating food stores, and damaging structures.
habitat: Sewers, drains, kitchens, grain stores, ceiling voids, gardens
diet: Omnivorous — grains, fruits, meat, garbage, wiring insulation
lifecycle: Gestation 21 days, 6-12 pups per litter, 5-10 litters/year. Sexually mature at 5 weeks.
india_distribution: Entire country, highest density in urban and agricultural areas
hadith_citation: Sahih al-Bukhari 1828, 3314; Sahih Muslim 1198
hadith_arabic: خَمْسٌ مِنَ الدَّوَابِّ كُلُّهُنَّ فَاسِقٌ يُقْتَلْنَ فِي الْحَرَمِ: الْغُرَابُ وَالْحِدَأَةُ وَالْعَقْرَبُ وَالْفَأْرَةُ وَالْكَلْبُ الْعَقُورُ
hadith_translation: Five are the vicious and harmful creatures which may be killed even in the state of Ihram: the crow, the kite, the scorpion, the mouse/rat, and the rabid dog.
quran_reference: Surah Al-An'am 6:38

madhab_rulings.hanafi: One of the five Fawasiq. Killing is permissible at all times including Ihram. No fidyah.
madhab_rulings.maliki: Explicitly named as fasiq by the Prophet ﷺ. Killing is permissible and encouraged.
madhab_rulings.shafii: Part of the five Fawasiq. May be killed in Haram, during Salah, and at all other times.
madhab_rulings.hanbali: Named by the Prophet ﷺ as fasiq. Killing permissible. Rat is specifically called 'Fuwaysiqah' (little fasiq).

incidence_rate: India: 10,000+ leptospirosis cases/year
prevention_methods: Store food in metal or thick plastic containers
Seal all holes >6mm in walls, pipes, and foundations
Fix leaking pipes (rats need water daily)
Keep garbage in sealed metal bins
Remove outdoor food sources (bird feeders, pet food)
Trim vegetation away from house walls
removal_methods: Snap traps (most humane mechanical method)
Live traps with relocation >2 km away
Rodenticide bait stations (tamper-resistant, away from children/pets)
Professional pest control for large infestations
PROHIBITED: Do not use glue traps (inhumane)
PROHIBITED: Do not poison without bait stations (secondary poisoning risk)

spiritual_protection.dua: أَعُوذُ بِكَلِمَاتِ اللَّهِ التَّامَّاتِ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا خَلَقَ
spiritual_protection.dua_translation: I seek refuge in the perfect words of Allah from the evil of what He has created.
spiritual_protection.prophetic_practice: The Prophet ﷺ specifically named the rat as 'Fuwaysiqah' and instructed that it be killed. He also warned that rats can pull candle wicks and start fires (Sahih al-Bukhari 3316).

climate_future: Urban flooding from extreme rainfall events pushes rats into homes and increases leptospirosis outbreaks. Warmer winters increase rat survival and breeding. Rising sea levels and coastal flooding will intensify urban rat problems in Mumbai, Kolkata, and Chennai.

faq.q: Why is the rat specifically named as Fawasiq?
faq.a: The Prophet ﷺ called it 'Fuwaysiqah' (little fasiq/harmful one) because of its destructive nature — it gnaws, contaminates food, spreads disease, and can start fires by dragging candle wicks.

faq.q: What is the most humane way to deal with rats?
faq.a: Prevention first (seal entry points, remove food sources). If trapping is needed, snap traps are considered most humane. Glue traps cause prolonged suffering and are discouraged.

faq.q: Can rats really start house fires?
faq.a: Yes. The Prophet ﷺ warned about this 1400 years ago (Sahih al-Bukhari 3316). Rats gnaw electrical wiring, causing an estimated 25% of unexplained house fires worldwide.

faq.q: What diseases do rats carry in India?
faq.a: Major risks include Leptospirosis (especially during monsoon floods in Mumbai, Kerala), Plague (historically), Hantavirus, Rat-bite fever, and Salmonellosis.

faq.q: How fast do rats reproduce?
faq.a: A single pair can produce up to 1,250 offspring in one year. Rats are sexually mature at 5 weeks and can have a new litter every 3 weeks.

emergency_contacts.nearest_antivenom: N/A — Antibiotics for leptospirosis at government hospitals

brief: Silent destroyers causing ₹35,000+ crore in annual property damage across India. They can compromise structural integrity of buildings.
description: Termites are social insects that feed on cellulose (wood, paper, fabric). In India, subterranean termites (Odontotermes, Coptotermes) cause catastrophic structural damage, often undetected until severe. They destroy wooden structures, books, documents, and can compromise building foundations. India loses an estimated ₹35,000 crore annually to termite damage.
size: Workers: 3-5 mm; Queens: up to 10 cm
habitat: Underground colonies, inside wooden structures, walls, foundations
diet: Cellulose — wood, paper, cardboard, fabric, books
lifecycle: Queen can live 25-50 years, laying thousands of eggs daily. Colony size: millions.
india_distribution: Entire country, especially problematic in tropical and subtropical regions
hadith_citation: The termite (Aradah) is mentioned in the Qur'an in relation to Prophet Sulaiman (عليه السلام). Killing for property protection is permissible.
hadith_arabic: لَا ضَرَرَ وَلَا ضِرَارَ
hadith_translation: There should be neither harm nor reciprocal harm. (Sunan Ibn Majah 2340)
quran_reference: Surah Saba 34:14 — 'Then when We decreed death for him [Sulaiman], nothing indicated his death to them except a creature of the earth (termite/woodworm) eating his staff.'

madhab_rulings.hanafi: Permissible to kill when they damage property. Property protection is a maqasid.
madhab_rulings.maliki: Permissible to kill. Preventing property destruction is a Shariah objective.
madhab_rulings.shafii: Permissible to kill to prevent harm (la darar wa la dirar).
madhab_rulings.hanbali: Permissible to kill. Protection of property (hifz al-mal) is one of the five maqasid.

health_risks: No direct health risks to humans
Structural collapse risk from compromised buildings
Damage to books, documents, and cultural heritage
fatality_india: Indirect: structural collapses from termite-weakened buildings
fatality_world: Indirect: property damage estimated at $40 billion/year globally
incidence_rate: Present in virtually all regions of India
high_risk_states: Highest damage in humid tropical regions
prevention_methods: Pre-construction anti-termite soil treatment
Use termite-resistant wood (teak, sal) for construction
Maintain ventilation in sub-floor areas
Keep wood, paper, and cardboard away from ground contact
Fix water leaks immediately (termites need moisture)
Annual termite inspections by professionals
removal_methods: Professional soil treatment with termiticides
Baiting systems (monitoring and colony elimination)
Physical barriers during construction
Heat treatment for localized infestations
PROHIBITED: Do not use banned pesticides (chlordane, etc.)
PROHIBITED: Do not attempt DIY treatment for severe infestations

spiritual_protection.dua: أَعُوذُ بِكَلِمَاتِ اللَّهِ التَّامَّاتِ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا خَلَقَ
spiritual_protection.dua_translation: I seek refuge in the perfect words of Allah from the evil of what He has created.
spiritual_protection.prophetic_practice: Protect property and documents. The termite is a reminder of the transience of worldly power (story of Prophet Sulaiman عليه السلام).

climate_future: Rising temperatures and increased humidity are expanding termite ranges and accelerating colony growth. Urban construction using wood and paper products provides abundant food. Climate change may increase termite biomass by 20-40% in South Asia by 2050.

faq.q: How do I know if I have termites?
faq.a: Look for: mud tubes on walls/foundations, hollow-sounding wood, discarded wings near windows, frass (termite droppings), and sagging floors or doors.

faq.q: Why are termites mentioned in the Qur'an?
faq.a: Surah Saba 34:14 mentions the termite eating Prophet Sulaiman's staff, revealing his death. It demonstrates that even the mightiest kingdom can be brought low by the smallest creature.

faq.q: Can I treat termites myself?
faq.a: Minor surface infestations can be treated with boric acid or orange oil. Severe infestations require professional treatment with soil injection or baiting systems.

faq.q: How much damage can termites cause?
faq.a: A mature colony can consume 5 kg of wood per day. In India, termites cause ₹35,000+ crore in annual property damage.

faq.q: Is it a sin to kill termites?
faq.a: No. Protection of property (hifz al-mal) is one of the five objectives of Shariah. Killing termites to protect buildings and possessions is permissible.

emergency_contacts.emergency: Local pest control services / Municipal Corporation

brief: Large ant species that excavates wood for nesting, causing structural damage similar to termites. Distinguished from regular ants which are protected.
description: Carpenter ants (Camponotus spp.) are large ants that excavate wood to create nesting galleries. Unlike termites, they don't eat wood but tunnel through it, weakening structural timbers, door frames, and furniture. They are distinguished from regular ants in Islamic jurisprudence — while regular ants are protected (based on the Qur'anic story), carpenter ants that damage property may be controlled.
habitat: Moist or decaying wood, tree stumps, wooden structures, window frames
diet: Insects, honeydew, sweets, proteins — they do NOT eat wood
lifecycle: Colony establishment takes 3-6 years. Mature colonies: 10,000-50,000 workers. Queen lives 10-15 years.
india_distribution: Throughout India, especially in forested and humid regions
hadith_citation: Distinguished from regular ants. Property protection ruling applies.
hadith_arabic: لَا ضَرَرَ وَلَا ضِرَارَ
hadith_translation: There should be neither harm nor reciprocal harm.
quran_reference: Surah An-Naml 27:18 — Regular ants are mentioned with respect. Carpenter ants distinguished by scholars as property-damaging species.

madhab_rulings.hanafi: May be controlled when causing property damage. Regular ants should not be killed unnecessarily.
madhab_rulings.maliki: Permissible to control for property protection. Distinguished from beneficial ant species.
madhab_rulings.shafii: May be killed if causing significant property damage. Regular ants are makruh to kill.
madhab_rulings.hanbali: Property protection justifies control. Scholars distinguish between harmful and beneficial ant species.

health_risks: Indicator of moisture problems in buildings
incidence_rate: Common in wooden structures across tropical India
prevention_methods: Fix all water leaks and moisture problems
Replace damaged or decaying wood promptly
Keep firewood and lumber away from house
Trim tree branches touching the house
Seal cracks and openings in the foundation
Ensure proper drainage around the building
removal_methods: Locate and treat the nest directly (often in wall voids)
Boric acid bait stations
Professional treatment of wall voids with dust formulations
Replace severely damaged wood
PROHIBITED: Do not use fire
PROHIBITED: Do not use broad-spectrum pesticides that harm beneficial insects

spiritual_protection.dua: أَعُوذُ بِكَلِمَاتِ اللَّهِ التَّامَّاتِ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا خَلَقَ
spiritual_protection.dua_translation: I seek refuge in the perfect words of Allah from the evil of what He has created.
spiritual_protection.surah: Surah An-Naml 27:18 (the story of Prophet Sulaiman and the ants)
spiritual_protection.prophetic_practice: The Prophet ﷺ prohibited killing ants without cause. Carpenter ants that damage property are distinguished from regular ants.

climate_future: Increased rainfall and humidity from climate change favor carpenter ant populations. Urban wooden construction and poor maintenance increase infestation risk.

faq.q: How are carpenter ants different from termites?
faq.a: Carpenter ants excavate wood for nesting but don't eat it. Termites eat wood. Carpenter ant damage shows smooth, clean galleries; termite damage shows mud-filled tunnels.

faq.q: Are carpenter ants the same as regular ants in Islamic ruling?
faq.a: No. Regular ants are mentioned respectfully in Surah An-Naml and are generally protected. Carpenter ants that damage property may be controlled under the property protection principle.

faq.q: How do I know if I have carpenter ants?
faq.a: Look for: sawdust piles (frass) near wood, large black ants (6-25mm), rustling sounds in walls, and winged ants emerging in spring.

faq.q: Can carpenter ants destroy my house?
faq.a: Over years, untreated infestations can cause significant structural damage. They prefer moist or decaying wood but will tunnel through sound wood as colonies grow.

faq.q: What attracts carpenter ants?
faq.a: Moisture is the primary attractant. Fix leaks, improve drainage, and replace decaying wood to make your home less attractive to carpenter ants.

brief: Urban pest causing property damage through acidic droppings, carrying respiratory diseases (Cryptococcosis, Histoplasmosis), and harboring ectoparasites.
description: Feral pigeons (Columba livia domestica) are among the most problematic urban pests in India. Their acidic droppings corrode building facades, solar panels, and vehicles. They carry respiratory pathogens (Cryptococcus, Histoplasma, Chlamydia psittaci), harbor mites and lice, and their nesting materials block drains and ventilation. Ethical deterrence is preferred over killing.
habitat: Building ledges, AC units, balconies, temple/mosque rooftops, railway stations
diet: Grains, seeds, food scraps, garbage
lifecycle: 2 eggs per clutch, 5-6 clutches/year, 17-19 day incubation. Lifespan 3-5 years (urban).
india_distribution: All urban areas across India
hadith_citation: Not among the five Fawasiq. Deterrence preferred over killing.
hadith_arabic: لَا ضَرَرَ وَلَا ضِرَارَ
hadith_translation: There should be neither harm nor reciprocal harm.
quran_reference: Birds mentioned positively in many verses. Pigeons held cultural significance in Islamic tradition (story of the cave during Hijrah).

madhab_rulings.hanafi: Not from the Fawasiq. Deterrence and exclusion preferred. Killing permissible only if causing significant harm and no alternative exists.
madhab_rulings.maliki: Deterrence preferred. May be controlled humanely if causing property damage or health risks.
madhab_rulings.shafii: Not inherently fasiq. Exclusion methods preferred. Killing as last resort for health protection.
madhab_rulings.hanbali: Deterrence and structural exclusion preferred. Killing permissible only when other methods fail and harm is significant.

health_risks: Cryptococcosis (fungal lung infection from dried droppings)
Pigeon mites and lice transferring to humans
fatality_india: Rare direct fatalities; immunocompromised individuals at risk from Cryptococcosis
incidence_rate: Pigeon-related respiratory diseases underreported in India
prevention_methods: Install bird spikes on ledges and AC units
Use bird netting to block nesting areas
Seal openings to attics and roof spaces
Remove food sources (don't feed pigeons on balconies)
Use reflective deterrents or ultrasonic devices
Keep balconies and terraces clean of droppings
removal_methods: Professional bird exclusion (netting, spikes, wire systems)
One-way exclusion doors on nesting areas
OvoControl (pigeon birth control — available in some cities)
Habitat modification (removing nesting sites)
PROHIBITED: Do not use poison (kills non-target birds)
PROHIBITED: Do not destroy eggs unless legally permitted

spiritual_protection.dua: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ
spiritual_protection.dua_translation: In the name of Allah.
spiritual_protection.surah: Surah An-Nahl 16:79 — 'Do they not see the birds controlled in the atmosphere of the sky?'
spiritual_protection.prophetic_practice: The pigeon is associated with the story of the Hijrah cave. While cultural reverence exists, health and property protection take priority through humane exclusion.

climate_future: Urban heat islands favor pigeon populations. Climate change may alter food availability and breeding patterns. Urbanization continues to provide nesting habitats.

faq.q: Are pigeons really a health risk?
faq.a: Yes. Dried pigeon droppings contain Cryptococcus and Histoplasma fungi that cause serious respiratory infections, especially dangerous for immunocompromised individuals.

faq.q: Is it a sin to kill pigeons in Islam?
faq.a: Pigeons are not among the five Fawasiq. Scholars recommend deterrence and exclusion first. Killing is permitted only as a last resort when they cause significant harm and no alternative works.

faq.q: Why do pigeons love AC units?
faq.a: AC units provide warmth, shelter from rain and predators, and flat surfaces for nesting. Install bird spikes or netting around units.

faq.q: How do I clean pigeon droppings safely?
faq.a: Wet the droppings first (to prevent spore inhalation), wear an N95 mask and gloves, use a scraper and disinfectant, and dispose of waste in sealed bags.

faq.q: Is feeding pigeons harmful?
faq.a: Yes, when done in residential areas. It concentrates pigeon populations, increases droppings, disease transmission, and property damage. Feed birds in open parks if desired.

emergency_contacts.emergency: Municipal Corporation (pigeon control) / Local pest control

brief: One of the five Fawasiq. India accounts for 36% of global rabies deaths. The 'rabid/aggressive dog' (al-kalb al-'aqur) is specifically named in Hadith.
description: The 'rabid/biting dog' (al-kalb al-'aqur) is one of the five creatures explicitly named by the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ as fasiq. India has the highest rabies burden globally — approximately 20,000 deaths annually, mostly from stray dog bites. The Islamic ruling specifically targets aggressive, rabid, or dangerous dogs, not all dogs. Modern management emphasizes Animal Birth Control (ABC) and mass vaccination.
size: Variable (medium-sized, 10-25 kg typically for Indian strays)
habitat: Streets, garbage dumps, markets, residential neighborhoods
diet: Omnivorous scavengers — garbage, food waste, small animals
lifecycle: Gestation 63 days, 4-6 pups per litter, 2 litters/year. Lifespan 3-5 years (street).
india_distribution: Estimated 35-60 million stray dogs across India
hadith_citation: Sahih al-Bukhari 1828; Sahih Muslim 1198
hadith_arabic: خَمْسٌ مِنَ الدَّوَابِّ كُلُّهُنَّ فَاسِقٌ يُقْتَلْنَ فِي الْحَرَمِ: الْغُرَابُ وَالْحِدَأَةُ وَالْعَقْرَبُ وَالْفَأْرَةُ وَالْكَلْبُ الْعَقُورُ
hadith_translation: Five are the vicious and harmful creatures which may be killed even in the state of Ihram: the crow, the kite, the scorpion, the mouse, and the rabid/aggressive dog.
quran_reference: Surah Al-Kahf 18:18, 22 — Dogs mentioned as companions of the People of the Cave. Surah Al-A'raf 7:176 — metaphorical reference.

madhab_rulings.hanafi: Al-kalb al-'aqur (rabid/biting dog) may be killed. Regular stray dogs should be managed humanely. Pet dogs are controversial but the aggressive/rabid specification is clear.
madhab_rulings.maliki: The 'aqur (aggressive/rabid) dog may be killed for public safety. ABC and vaccination preferred for stray population management.
madhab_rulings.shafii: Specific to al-kalb al-'aqur. Not a blanket ruling on all dogs. Rabid and aggressive dogs threatening human safety may be killed.
madhab_rulings.hanbali: The ruling is specific to dogs that bite/attack ('aqur). Harmless strays should be managed through humane methods.

incidence_rate: India: 17.4 million dog bites/year
prevention_methods: Avoid approaching or feeding unknown stray dogs
Do not run from an aggressive dog (back away slowly)
Keep children supervised in areas with stray dogs
Support municipal ABC (Animal Birth Control) programs
Report aggressive/rabid dogs to municipal authorities
Ensure all pet dogs are vaccinated against rabies
removal_methods: Report rabid/aggressive dogs to municipal animal control
Support ABC (Animal Birth Control) and mass vaccination programs
Municipal authorities handle dangerous dog situations
Post-bite: Wash wound with soap and water for 15 minutes, then seek immediate medical attention
PROHIBITED: Do not use poison (kills non-target animals)
PROHIBITED: Do not use cruel methods (shooting, beating in public)

spiritual_protection.dua: أَعُوذُ بِكَلِمَاتِ اللَّهِ التَّامَّاتِ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا خَلَقَ
spiritual_protection.dua_translation: I seek refuge in the perfect words of Allah from the evil of what He has created.
spiritual_protection.surah: Recite Ayat al-Kursi and morning/evening adhkar
spiritual_protection.prophetic_practice: The Hadith specifies 'al-kalb al-'aqur' — the aggressive/rabid dog. The Prophet ﷺ also narrated the story of a person forgiven for giving water to a thirsty dog (Sahih al-Bukhari 2363), showing compassion to non-threatening dogs.

climate_future: Climate change and urbanization are increasing human-stray dog conflicts. Warmer temperatures may affect rabies virus dynamics. Urban expansion into rural areas increases encounters with unvaccinated dog populations.

faq.q: Does Islam say to kill all dogs?
faq.a: No. The Hadith specifically mentions 'al-kalb al-'aqur' — the rabid or aggressive biting dog. The Prophet ﷺ also narrated rewards for showing kindness to dogs (Sahih al-Bukhari 2363).

faq.q: What should I do if bitten by a dog?
faq.a: Immediately wash the wound with soap and running water for 15 minutes. Go to the nearest hospital for PEP (Post-Exposure Prophylaxis) — a series of rabies vaccines. Do NOT delay. Rabies is 99.9% fatal once symptoms appear.

faq.q: Is there a cure for rabies?
faq.a: There is no cure once symptoms appear. Prevention through PEP (vaccines) after a bite is the only option. Pre-exposure vaccination is available for high-risk individuals.

faq.q: What is ABC (Animal Birth Control)?
faq.a: A humane program where stray dogs are captured, sterilized, vaccinated against rabies, and released. It is the WHO-recommended method for stray dog population management.

faq.q: How can I tell if a dog is rabid?
faq.a: Signs include: excessive drooling, aggression, difficulty walking, paralysis, biting at imaginary objects, and fear of water. Report suspected rabid dogs to municipal authorities immediately.

emergency_contacts.nearest_antivenom: PEP (Rabies Vaccine) available at government hospitals, PHCs

sources: Association for Prevention and Control of Rabies in India (APCRI)

brief: Reservoir hosts for Nipah virus (40-75% fatality rate) and other deadly zoonotic viruses. Kerala has experienced multiple Nipah outbreaks linked to fruit bats.
description: Bats, particularly fruit bats (Pteropus medius), are reservoir hosts for some of the most dangerous viruses known — Nipah (40-75% fatality), Rabies, Coronaviruses, and Hendra. Kerala has experienced multiple Nipah outbreaks linked to Indian Flying Fox bats. While ecologically vital as pollinators and seed dispersers, bats roosting in or near homes pose significant zoonotic risk.
size: Wingspan up to 1.5 m (Indian Flying Fox)
habitat: Large trees (banyan, fig), caves, abandoned buildings, roof spaces
diet: Fruit bats: fruits, nectar, pollen. Insectivorous bats: insects
lifecycle: Usually 1 pup/year. Lifespan 20-30 years. Colonial roosting (hundreds to thousands).
india_distribution: Throughout India, Indian Flying Fox concentrated in South and West India
hadith_citation: Classified under harmful creatures when entering homes
hadith_arabic: مَا لَا يُؤْذِي فَلَا يُقْتَلُ وَمَا يُؤْذِي فَيُقْتَلُ
hadith_translation: That which does not harm should not be killed, and that which harms may be killed.
quran_reference: General ecological principles — Surah Al-An'am 6:38

madhab_rulings.hanafi: Exclusion from homes preferred. Killing permissible if they pose disease risk.
madhab_rulings.maliki: Humane exclusion recommended. Killing permissible for health protection.
madhab_rulings.shafii: Not from the Fawasiq. Exclusion preferred. Killing as last resort for disease prevention.
madhab_rulings.hanbali: Deterrence and exclusion preferred. Killing permissible when they pose danger to health.

health_risks: Nipah virus (40-75% fatality, no vaccine, no treatment)
fatality_india: Nipah outbreaks: Kerala 2018 (17 deaths), 2019 (1 death), 2021 (1 death), 2023 (2 deaths)
incidence_rate: Nipah outbreaks sporadic but devastating; bat-human contact increasing
prevention_methods: Do not consume fruits with bite marks (potential Nipah transmission)
Cover fruit trees with netting
Seal openings to attics and roof spaces
Install one-way exclusion devices at roosting entries
Avoid contact with bat droppings or urine
Do not handle dead or sick bats
removal_methods: Professional bat exclusion at dusk (when bats leave to feed)
One-way exclusion devices on entry points
Seal all gaps >1.5 cm after confirming bats have left
Clean guano with wet method (N95 mask, gloves, disinfectant)
PROHIBITED: Do not trap or handle bats (rabies/Nipah risk)
PROHIBITED: Do not use poison or fumigation

spiritual_protection.dua: أَعُوذُ بِكَلِمَاتِ اللَّهِ التَّامَّاتِ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا خَلَقَ
spiritual_protection.dua_translation: I seek refuge in the perfect words of Allah from the evil of what He has created.
spiritual_protection.surah: Morning and evening adhkar for protection
spiritual_protection.prophetic_practice: Maintain distance from wildlife. Seal homes to prevent entry of wild creatures.

climate_future: Deforestation and urbanization are pushing bats closer to human settlements. Climate change alters fruit availability, causing bats to forage in populated areas. Nipah spillover events expected to increase with habitat encroachment.

faq.q: What is Nipah virus?
faq.a: A bat-borne virus with 40-75% fatality rate. Transmitted through contaminated fruit, bat secretions, or person-to-person contact. No vaccine or specific treatment exists. Kerala has had multiple outbreaks.

faq.q: Should I kill bats near my home?
faq.a: Exclusion is strongly preferred over killing. Bats are crucial pollinators and insect controllers. Professional exclusion during non-breeding season is the recommended approach.

faq.q: Are bats protected by law in India?
faq.a: Yes. Indian Flying Fox is listed under Schedule V of the Wildlife Protection Act 1972. Killing requires specific permission. Exclusion and deterrence are legally preferred.

faq.q: How does Nipah virus spread?
faq.a: Through bat saliva/urine on fruits → human consumption. Also person-to-person through respiratory droplets and bodily fluids. Healthcare workers are at high risk during outbreaks.

faq.q: What should I do if I find a dead bat?
faq.a: Do not touch it. Contact local animal/health authorities. If you must handle it, use thick gloves and place in a sealed bag. Report to the nearest health centre.

emergency_contacts.nearest_antivenom: N/A — No specific Nipah treatment. Supportive care at isolation facilities.

brief: The Prophet ﷺ specifically commanded killing the gecko (wazagh), calling it 'Fuwaysiq' (little harmful one). Believed to have blown on Prophet Ibrahim's fire.
description: The house gecko (wazagh) holds a unique position in Islamic jurisprudence — the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ specifically commanded killing it and called it 'Fuwaysiqah' (little harmful creature). The Hadith states it blew on the fire of Prophet Ibrahim (عليه السلام). Scientifically, geckos can carry Salmonella and contaminate food, though they also eat insects. The Hadith ruling is clear despite their perceived benefit.
habitat: Walls, ceilings, behind picture frames, near light fixtures (attracted to insects)
diet: Insects — mosquitoes, moths, cockroaches, spiders
lifecycle: 2 eggs per clutch, multiple clutches/year. Lifespan 5-7 years.
india_distribution: Entire country, ubiquitous in all human dwellings
hadith_citation: Sahih Muslim 2237, 2238; Sahih al-Bukhari 3359
hadith_arabic: مَنْ قَتَلَ وَزَغَةً فِي أَوَّلِ ضَرْبَةٍ كُتِبَ لَهُ كَذَا وَكَذَا حَسَنَةً
hadith_translation: Whoever kills a gecko with the first blow will have such and such number of good deeds written for him.
quran_reference: Referenced in relation to Prophet Ibrahim's trial of fire — Surah Al-Anbiya 21:68-69

madhab_rulings.hanafi: Killing the wazagh (gecko) is recommended (mustahabb) based on explicit Hadith. Reward mentioned for first-strike killing.
madhab_rulings.maliki: Recommended to kill based on the Prophet's explicit instruction. Called 'Fuwaysiqah' by the Prophet ﷺ.
madhab_rulings.shafii: Killing is recommended (sunnah). The Hadith specifically names the wazagh and promises reward for killing it.
madhab_rulings.hanbali: Killing is recommended based on multiple authentic Hadiths. The gecko is classified as fasiq.

health_risks: Salmonella transmission through droppings on food
incidence_rate: Present in virtually every Indian household
prevention_methods: Seal cracks and gaps around doors, windows, and pipes
Reduce insect populations (geckos follow their prey)
Use mesh screens on windows
Keep lights off near entry points at night
Use naphthalene balls or eggshells as natural deterrents
Seal food containers to prevent contamination
removal_methods: Capture with a box and cardboard and release outdoors
Use cardboard tube traps
Reduce insect prey to discourage habitation
Commercial gecko repellent sprays
PROHIBITED: Do not use glue traps (inhumane)
PROHIBITED: Do not use pesticides that harm other beneficial creatures

spiritual_protection.dua: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ
spiritual_protection.dua_translation: In the name of Allah.
spiritual_protection.prophetic_practice: The Prophet ﷺ explicitly commanded killing the wazagh (Sahih Muslim 2237). He said it blew on the fire of Ibrahim (عليه السلام). Killing it in the first strike carries the highest reward.

climate_future: Climate change and urbanization increase insect populations, which in turn support larger gecko populations. Geckos are highly adaptable and will continue to thrive in human environments.

faq.q: Why did the Prophet ﷺ command killing geckos?
faq.a: The Hadith states the gecko blew on the fire of Prophet Ibrahim (عليه السلام) to make it burn more. The Prophet ﷺ called it 'Fuwaysiqah' and promised reward for killing it (Sahih Muslim 2237-2238).

faq.q: Aren't geckos beneficial because they eat mosquitoes?
faq.a: While geckos do eat insects, the Hadith ruling is clear and takes precedence. Scholars note that the ruling is specific to the wazagh and may reflect knowledge beyond what is apparent.

faq.q: Is there extra reward for killing a gecko in one strike?
faq.a: Yes. The Hadith mentions specific rewards: first strike gets the highest reward, second strike less, and third strike less still (Sahih Muslim 2238).

faq.q: Do I have to kill every gecko I see?
faq.a: The ruling is 'recommended' (mustahabb/sunnah), not obligatory (wajib). It is meritorious to do so but not sinful to leave them.

faq.q: Can geckos contaminate food?
faq.a: Yes. Gecko droppings can contain Salmonella bacteria. Contamination of uncovered food and cooking surfaces is a real health concern.

brief: PROTECTED — The Prophet ﷺ explicitly prohibited killing frogs. Their croaking is considered tasbih (glorification of Allah).
description: Frogs hold a unique protected status in Islam. The Prophet Muhammad ﷺ explicitly prohibited killing frogs, stating that their croaking is tasbih (glorification of Allah). A physician asked the Prophet ﷺ about using frogs in medicine, and he forbade killing them (Sunan Abu Dawud 3871, Sunan An-Nasa'i 4355). Ecologically, frogs are critical pest controllers, consuming vast quantities of mosquitoes and other harmful insects.
habitat: Near water bodies, wetlands, gardens, rice paddies
diet: Insects, mosquitoes, worms, small invertebrates
lifecycle: Eggs → Tadpole → Froglet → Adult. 12-16 week metamorphosis.
india_distribution: Throughout India, highest diversity in Western Ghats and Northeast
hadith_citation: Sunan Abu Dawud 3871; Sunan An-Nasa'i 4355
hadith_arabic: نَهَى رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ عَنْ قَتْلِ الضِّفْدَعِ
hadith_translation: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ prohibited the killing of frogs.
quran_reference: Surah Al-An'am 6:38 — All creatures are communities. Frogs' croaking is considered tasbih.

madhab_rulings.hanafi: PROHIBITED to kill. Explicit Hadith prohibition. Their croaking is tasbih.
madhab_rulings.maliki: PROHIBITED to kill. Based on clear Prophetic prohibition.
madhab_rulings.shafii: PROHIBITED to kill. The Prophet ﷺ forbade a physician from using frogs in medicine.
madhab_rulings.hanbali: PROHIBITED to kill. Unanimous agreement on the prohibition based on authentic Hadith.

health_risks: No significant health risks to humans
Beneficial: consume mosquitoes and agricultural pests
Some species have mild skin toxins (not dangerous to humans in India)
incidence_rate: Declining populations due to habitat loss and pollution
prevention_methods: No prevention needed — frogs are beneficial
If frogs enter the home, gently relocate them outside
Maintain garden water features to attract frogs (natural pest control)
Avoid using pesticides that harm frog populations
Support wetland conservation
Create frog-friendly habitat in gardens
removal_methods: Gently capture and relocate outdoors
Guide frogs toward doors/windows
Remove attractants (standing water near doors if unwanted)
PROHIBITED: Do not kill — explicit Prophetic prohibition
PROHIBITED: Do not use pesticides or chemicals
PROHIBITED: Do not harm or injure

spiritual_protection.dua: سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ
spiritual_protection.dua_translation: Glory be to Allah (recognizing the frog's tasbih).
spiritual_protection.surah: Surah Al-Isra 17:44 — 'There is not a thing that does not glorify His praise, but you do not understand their glorification.'
spiritual_protection.prophetic_practice: The Prophet ﷺ prohibited killing frogs and said their croaking is tasbih. When you hear frogs, remember this is dhikr of Allah.

climate_future: Frogs are among the most climate-vulnerable animals. One-third of amphibian species are threatened. Habitat loss, pollution, chytrid fungus, and climate change are causing global declines. Protecting frogs aligns with both Islamic and ecological principles.

faq.q: Why can't we kill frogs in Islam?
faq.a: The Prophet ﷺ explicitly prohibited killing frogs (Sunan Abu Dawud 3871). He said their croaking is tasbih (glorification of Allah). This is a clear, unambiguous prohibition.

faq.q: Can frogs be used in traditional medicine?
faq.a: No. A physician asked the Prophet ﷺ about this, and he specifically prohibited using frogs in medicine (Sunan An-Nasa'i 4355).

faq.q: Are frogs beneficial?
faq.a: Extremely. A single frog can eat thousands of mosquitoes and agricultural pests in its lifetime. They are natural, chemical-free pest control.

faq.q: What if a frog enters my house?
faq.a: Gently capture it (or guide it) and release it outside near a water source. Do not harm it.

faq.q: Why are frogs declining globally?
faq.a: Habitat destruction, pollution, pesticide use, climate change, and the chytrid fungus are causing a global amphibian extinction crisis. Protecting frogs is both an Islamic and ecological imperative.

brief: PROTECTED — Named in the Qur'an (Surah An-Naml) as the bird of Prophet Sulaiman (عليه السلام). The Prophet ﷺ prohibited killing hoopoes.
description: The Hoopoe (Hudhud) is one of the most revered birds in Islam. It is named in the Qur'an as the bird that served Prophet Sulaiman (عليه السلام), bringing him news of the Queen of Sheba. The Prophet Muhammad ﷺ explicitly prohibited killing hoopoes (Sunan Abu Dawud 5267, Sunan Ibn Majah 3224). Ecologically, hoopoes are beneficial insectivores that control soil pests.
habitat: Open grasslands, orchards, gardens, parks
diet: Insects, larvae, grubs, small reptiles extracted from soil with long bill
lifecycle: 5-8 eggs per clutch, 15-18 day incubation. Lifespan 10 years.
india_distribution: Throughout India, migratory in northern regions
hadith_citation: Sunan Abu Dawud 5267; Sunan Ibn Majah 3224
hadith_arabic: نَهَى رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ عَنْ قَتْلِ أَرْبَعٍ مِنَ الدَّوَابِّ: النَّمْلَةِ وَالنَّحْلَةِ وَالْهُدْهُدِ وَالصُّرَدِ
hadith_translation: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ prohibited the killing of four creatures: the ant, the bee, the hoopoe, and the shrike.
quran_reference: Surah An-Naml 27:20-28 — The story of Prophet Sulaiman and the Hoopoe (Hudhud).

madhab_rulings.hanafi: PROHIBITED to kill. Explicit Hadith prohibition. Named in the Qur'an.
